_meta.description: Bootstrap eval set for the Oracle reranker bake-off (Task 14). SYNTHETIC — these query/doc pairs were hand-crafted to cover the four scenario dimensions named in the plan. Replace with real labeled data once RESOLVE-phase lessons accumulate enough volume (target: ≥50 query/doc pairs from real runs). Doc bodies are intentionally minimal; a real eval set would embed the full chunk text.

docs.lesson-worktree-001: Use isolation: worktree on Agent tool calls that mutate files in parallel. Unchanged worktrees are auto-removed.
docs.lesson-worktree-002: Parallel agents mutating the same file without worktree isolation cause merge conflicts. Always set isolation: worktree for multi-agent file writes.
docs.lesson-branch-protocol-003: All active runs must use a branch or worktree — no direct writes to main during a run.
docs.knowledge-git-ops-004: Git operations: git checkout -b creates a new branch. git worktree add creates an isolated working tree.
docs.lesson-council-005: Council review must precede PLAN exit when kernel or policy YAML is changed.

docs.knowledge-council-001: Council gate: any change to kernel code, policy YAML, or canonical docs requires council review before PLAN exits. Zero material findings must be unresolved.
docs.lesson-council-002: Skipping the council gate on a policy YAML change caused a silent regression in Guardian Layer-B. Always invoke uacp-council before PLAN exits on config/ changes.
docs.lesson-heartgate-003: Heartgate checks PLAN_VALIDATION ledger entry before allowing PLAN → EXECUTE transition.
docs.knowledge-evidence-004: Evidence must be produced. 'Done' without a backing artifact and ledger entry is a Heartgate blocker.
docs.lesson-worktree-005: Worktree cleanup is automatic for unchanged trees but must be explicit for trees with uncommitted changes.

entries.query: how does the Guardian classify tool calls and what happens when a tool is not in the allowed list for the current phase

docs.knowledge-guardian-classify-001: The Guardian classifies every tool call before allowing it. Classification sources (in priority order): [guardian.tool_classification] TOML table (explicit entry wins), provider heuristics (mcp__ prefix → mcp.*, write-pattern names → write.*), then runtime.extension fallback. The classification determines which Layer-B check applies.
docs.knowledge-guardian-layer-b-002: Layer-B enforcement: if the tool's classification is not in the phase's allowed_tools set, the Guardian emits an allowlist_miss block. Tools classified read.local bypass the allowlist check entirely — they are always permitted. Tools classified write.* require an explicit entry in allowed_tools for the phase.
docs.lesson-allowlist-miss-003: A custom plugin tool was blocked with allowlist_miss during propose because it lacked a classification entry. Fix: add tool_name = 'read.local' under [guardian.tool_classification] in uacp.toml.
docs.knowledge-heartgate-phase-004: Heartgate validates phase transitions, not individual tool calls. Guardian handles per-tool-call policy; Heartgate handles PHASE → PHASE boundary crossing.
docs.lesson-read-local-005: Read-only tools that query local state (oracle, config, file reads) should be classified read.local so Guardian Layer-B's 'reads pass' guard skips them without requiring an allowed_tools entry.

entries.query: what is the authority chain when layers conflict and how do I override a policy decision that was set in a higher-priority layer

docs.knowledge-authority-chain-001: UACP authority chain (earlier layers win): 1. docs/INDEX.md (document registry and canonical agent read order). 2. docs/ (intent, principles, lifecycle, policy). 3. config/ (machine-readable rules derived from docs). 4. state/ (current lifecycle position and run pointers). 5. skills/ (implement the documented rules). An explicit entry in docs/decisions/decision-log.md is the only mechanism to override this order.
docs.knowledge-decision-log-002: To override a higher-priority layer decision: record the decision in docs/decisions/decision-log.md with the date, the layers involved, the rationale, and the council finding (if any). The decision-log entry is the authoritative record — without it, the higher-priority layer wins.
docs.lesson-layer-conflict-003: Attempting to override a docs/ policy by editing only config/ failed silently. The docs/ layer won. Correct approach: update docs/ first, derive config/ from it, record in decision-log.md.
docs.knowledge-config-collapse-004: Config collapse: grammar from docs/ is codified to Python (engines/domain/) as the code default; project knobs live in uacp.toml. Doctrine (the 'why') stays in YAML docs. Never let config/ become the authority for doctrine.
docs.lesson-council-override-005: Council finding M-1 (doc, not block) was overridden without a decision-log entry. Audit caught the discrepancy at VERIFY. Always log M-1 resolutions in decision-log.md.
